title: Felicia westiae
taxonomy: Felicia westiae is classified within the Asteraceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This family includes more than 23,000 species, many of which are found in temperate and subtropical regions. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Felicia westiae shares characteristics with other daisy-like plants, such as composite flowers and a central disc of tiny flowers surrounded by larger, showy flowers. Felicia westiae is further classified within the tribe Gnaphalieae, which includes various daisy-like plants and shrubs. The specific classification of Felicia westiae within the Asteraceae family highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
